title: rubus hirtus var. pellitissimus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. pellitissimus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan wild raspberry, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. Traditionally, Rubus hirtus var. pellitissimus has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves, roots, and fruits have been used in herbal preparations to alleviate symptoms of these conditions, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting digestive health. Rubus hirtus var. pellitissimus can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its fruits can be eaten fresh or used to make jams and jellies.
